name: Tiered list
description: Tiered list is a pattern for presenting a list of paired titles and descriptions underneath a top-level title and description. It organises related items into a structured, scannable format using Tiered blocks as its primary content units. An optional [CTA block](/sites/construct/cta-block) can appear at the section level, below the top-level description.
usage:
Use Tiered list to present multiple related items that share a consistent structure of paired titles and descriptions. It works best for text-heavy content where each item is concise and parallel in nature.

### Layout

Tiered list adapts across screen sizes. On large screens, it uses a 6-column layout — 2 columns for the heading and 4 for the body, arranged in a single row. On medium screens, the layout is configurable: content can split(50/50) or stack vertically(full-width). On small screens, content always stacks.

### When to use

*   Content consists of paired titles and descriptions organised under a single top-level heading
*   Items are primarily text-based with short, parallel headings
*   Users need to scan a structured list quickly
*   Content represents steps, stages, features, or grouped concepts

### When not to use

*   Each item requires rich content such as images, videos, or code blocks — use [Divided section](/sites/construct/divided-section) instead
*   Content is brief with an image as the primary focus — use [Equal heights](/sites/construct/equal-heights) instead
*   The list contains only one or two items within a single topic — use a [Basic section](/sites/construct/basic-section) instead

table.name: Rule
table.description: The rule component indicates the beginning of a new section.

table.name: Heading
table.description: The section heading communicates the primary subject or theme of the section.

table.name: Body
table.description: A description that provides introductory context for the section. It appears at the top of the section and helps users understand the purpose or scope of the content before engaging with the content below.

table.name: CTA block
table.description: The [CTA block](wiki-ref://grid-ymtdOF6n4R/i-0Z_OLuJDCc) presents one or more related actions that guide the user toward the next step within a section

table.name: Tiered blocks
table.description: [Tiered block](wiki-ref://grid-ymtdOF6n4R/i-50G4VqVNjN) is a content unit that pairs a heading with a body to present a single item in a structured, scannable format.

properties.name: Section rule
properties.description: The rule component indicates the beginning of a new pattern
properties.constraint: Only use the default style for the section-level rule

properties.name: Linked heading
properties.description: The heading may function as a link when clearly indicated.

properties.name: Medium layout
properties.description: Indicate layout for the medium dimension

properties.name: Bottom padding
properties.description: The type of padding applied to the bottom of the pattern

properties.name: Linked heading
properties.description: The heading may function as a link when clearly indicated.

properties.name: Block rule
properties.description: The muted rule component indicates the beginning of a new block
properties.constraint: It is not used for the first block and appears from the second block onward.

properties.name: Image heading
properties.description: Image used in place of heading
properties.constraint: If an image is used as the heading, it cannot function as a link.

properties.name: Link CTA
properties.description: The text link used for contextual actions or pathways to more information. It uses a chevron (›) to indicate navigation to additional content or pages.

properties.name: Bottom padding
properties.description: The type of padding applied to the bottom of the pattern

properties.name: Primary CTA
properties.description: The CTA used with the positive button style. It indicates a high-priority, positive action and is reserved for hero areas or other key call-to-action sections.

properties.name: Secondary CTA
properties.description: The CTA using the default button style. It is used for most actions that are positive but not primary, and may serve as the main CTA in non-hero sections.

properties.name: Link CTA
properties.description: The text link used for contextual actions or pathways to more information. It uses a chevron (›) to indicate navigation to additional content or pages.
